meta.maintenanceInstruction: KEEP THE PLAN UP TO DATE AS YOU EVOLVE THE CODE. DEVIATIONS TO THE PLAN MUST BE LOGGED WITHIN THE PLAN. THE PLAN MUST BE REASSESSED HOLISTICALLY AFTER ANY CHANGE OF ANY SIZE.
meta.completionPolicy: YOU ARE NOT DONE UNTIL YOU HAVE COMPLETED 100% OF THE BLUEPRINT. DO NOT STOP. YOU MUST REFINE AT REGULAR INTERVALS TO KEEP THE BLUEPRINT FULL. NEVER SPECIFY ANY ESTIMATE OF EFFORT OR COST. NEVER SPECIFY A PRIORITY OF ANY KIND.
meta.blueprintRefinement: THIS BLUEPRINT MUST BE CONTINUALLY REFINED. After completing any task, before starting the next, re-read this file and assess whether it still accurately captures reality. If any task description is stale, wrong, unclear, or missing context — FIX IT NOW.

globalAlerts.warning: ON TOOLS: Use config.mk custom targets. Limit output. Pipe through tail after tee to build.log. Use 'make find-test-failures'.
CROSS-PLATFORM: ALL checks must pass on macOS, Linux (Docker), Windows (host 'moo'). Zero tolerance.
TIMING: Tests MUST NOT be timing-dependent. No long-running tests. Race detector must pass.
SESSION TIMER: Started 2026-02-20T08:21:58Z. 9h mandate. Verify: make check-session-time.
RULE OF TWO: Two contiguous issue-free subagent reviews before any commit. Same diff. No exceptions.
DEADCODE: NEVER add ignores to .deadcodeignore. Wire up the code or delete it.
BUILD: Run 'make make-all-with-log' after EVERY code change. Zero failures before proceeding.

mandatoryDirectives.contextManagement: One task at a time, in order. Do not attempt to solve all tasks in a single turn.
mandatoryDirectives.subagentPolicy: Use subagents for Rule of Two review gate ONLY. Work subagents do NOT self-review.
mandatoryDirectives.blueprintRefinement: Continual refinement required. After every task, re-read and fix stale descriptions.
mandatoryDirectives.runMakeAfterEveryChange: Run 'make make-all-with-log' after EVERY code change. Zero failures before proceeding.
mandatoryDirectives.commitPolicy: Commit in logical chunks after passing Rule of Two. Do NOT accumulate massive diffs.

statusSection.currentState: T101-T114 Done. Build GREEN. Proceeding to T115.
statusSection.priorCompletedWork: T001-T029 completed in prior sessions. See CHANGELOG.md and WIP.md for details. Removed unwired readable_stream.go (will be re-created properly in T105).

sequentialTasks.task: T101: Fix txtar context root formatting
sequentialTasks.description: Fixed in ctxutil.go buildContext(): metadata lines (context root, common path, tracked directories) are now extracted and rendered OUTSIDE the code fence with paths in backticks. 3 new test cases (MetadataExtracted, NoMetadata, MetadataOnly). Build GREEN.

sequentialTasks.task: T102: Fix txtar path ambiguity in computeUniqueSuffixes
sequentialTasks.description: VERIFIED: All 3 bug scenarios already fixed + tested in context_txtar_lca_test.go from prior sessions. (1) LCA computed and emitted in txtar Comment via computePathLCA — 7 test cases. (2) All-relative collision groups use full relative paths (allRelative check, false proximity fix) — 2 test cases. (3) Non-colliding basenames preserve full paths for relative entries — 2 test cases. Removed duplicate tests from state_coverage_gaps_test.go. Build GREEN.

sequentialTasks.task: T103: Fix MCP auto-config to use command/stdio instead of URL/HTTP
sequentialTasks.description: DONE: Rewrote MCPInstanceConfig to use command/args format (stdio transport) instead of URL/HTTP. Removed: ListenAndServe, Endpoint, ListenerAddr, ErrNotListening, ErrAlreadyListening, endpointType, HTTP server, Unix socket. Added: OsmBinary field (defaults to os.Executable()), command/args in mcpServerEntry. Updated JS bindings in module.go (removed HTTP methods, added osmBinary). Updated instance.go state writing. 15 tests covering new format. Build GREEN.

sequentialTasks.task: FIX POTENTIALLY MULTIPLE TESTS: 'test commit'
sequentialTasks.description: VERIFIED: No tests mutate the real repository. All 9 files with git operations use t.TempDir() for isolation. The 'test commit' strings in gitops_test.go and templates_test.go are commit messages for in-memory/temp repos. One test (TestCodeReviewCommand_ActualGitDiffExecution) reads from the real repo but is read-only (git diff). No fix needed.

sequentialTasks.task: T104: Implement osm mcp-instance subcommand (stdio MCP server)
sequentialTasks.description: DONE: Created mcp_instance.go with MCPInstanceCommand (--session flag, validation, reuses newMCPServer from mcp.go). Registered in main.go. 4 tests in mcp_instance_test.go (NewMCPInstanceCommand, SetupFlags, Execute_MissingSession, Execute_UnexpectedArgs) with stubGoalRegistry. Shell completion auto-inherited from command registry. Build GREEN, all packages pass.

sequentialTasks.task: T105: Implement ReadableStream type for fetch Response.body
sequentialTasks.description: DONE: Created readable_stream.go with ReadableStream (io.ReadCloser source, bounded channel pump, 64 KiB chunks, back-pressure) and ReadableStreamDefaultReader (Read, ReleaseLock). JS wrappers: wrapReadableStreamJS (locked getter, getReader, cancel), wrapReaderJS (read sync {value,done}, releaseLock). Wired into fetch.go buildResponse as response.body backed by buffered bytes. 15 Go-level unit tests in readable_stream_test.go. Build GREEN.

sequentialTasks.task: T106: Implement reader.read() with Promise resolution
sequentialTasks.description: DONE: Upgraded reader.read() to return Promise<{value: string, done: boolean}> using adapter.JS().NewChainedPromise(). Blocking Read() runs in goroutine, resolves on event loop via adapter.Loop().Submit(). Added adapter parameter threading through wrapReadableStreamJS→wrapReaderJS→buildResponse→jsFetch. 5 JS integration tests: GetReader_ReadAll, Locked, Cancel, EmptyBody, TextAndBodyIndependent. Build GREEN.

sequentialTasks.task: T107: Implement SSE parser for ReadableStream
sequentialTasks.description: DONE: Created sse.go with SSEParser (W3C SSE spec: double-newline delimiters, colon field parsing, comment lines, \r\n and \n, multi-line data, ID persistence, null byte rejection). JS wrapper wrapSSEParserJS with Promise-based read() returning {event, data, id}. Factory function sseReader(body) exposed on module exports using _goStream hidden property for Go↔JS bridge. 16 Go-level tests in sse_test.go. Build GREEN.

sequentialTasks.task: T108: End-to-end Goja integration tests for ReadableStream + SSE
sequentialTasks.description: DONE: 7 E2E JS tests added to fetch_test.go using httptest SSE servers: SSEReader_ParsesEvents (multi-event with custom types), SSEReader_MultiLineData, SSEReader_WithID, SSEReader_EmptyStream, ReadableStream_ChunkedBody (chunked encoding), ReadableStream_CancelMidStream. All use runAsync + testutil.TestEventLoopProvider. Build GREEN.

sequentialTasks.task: T109: macOS process hierarchy fallback for session ID
sequentialTasks.description: DONE: Created session_darwin.go (//go:build darwin) with Deep Anchor using sysctl via unix.SysctlKinfoProc for process tree traversal — DarwinProcInfo extraction, getBootSessionUUID, TTY via /dev/fd/N, findStableAnchorDarwin with skip/shell/root lists and terminal device boundary detection. Updated session_other.go+test build tag to !linux && !windows && !darwin. 30 tests in session_darwin_test.go. Updated docs (session.md hierarchy, sophisticated-auto-determination §3b). Build GREEN.

sequentialTasks.task: T110: Implement osm claudemux run — PTY subprocess spawning
sequentialTasks.description: DONE: Added 'run' subcommand to ClaudeMuxCommand. Per-task agent spawning via Provider interface (ClaudeCodeProvider wraps creack/pty). Pool used as concurrency limiter (worker slots). Task sources: positional args + --tasks-file (one per line, # comments). Signal handling (SIGINT/SIGTERM → context cancellation). Flags: --provider, --model, --dir, --command, --tasks-file. providerOverride field for test injection. dispatchTask creates fresh Instance+Agent per task. handleAgentExit for clean exit code reporting. 16 tests with mockProvider + echoAgent + failingAgent mocks. Build GREEN.

sequentialTasks.task: T111: Wire Pool dispatch into claudemux run
sequentialTasks.description: DONE: Wired ManagedSession health tracking into dispatchTask — each task gets Parser→Guard→Supervisor pipeline via ProcessLine(). Guard actions: escalate/timeout abort task, pause resumes. ProcessCrash() on non-zero exit. Guard event counting via *int pointer (avoids stale copy). Summary includes guard_events. Pool.Drain() in signal handler for graceful shutdown. Pool.WaitDrained() after task completion. CR-stripped line splitting for Windows PTY. 5 new tests + 2 updated (verboseAgent, crash reporting, mixed success/failure). Build GREEN.

sequentialTasks.task: Design orchestration IPC architecture
sequentialTasks.description: Create schema and transport mechanism (Unix domain sockets or similar) to allow CLI `submit` to push commands to a running background orchestrator daemon. REMEMBER: Unix domain sockets ARE preferred, but Windows must also be supported.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement Orchestrator Daemon
sequentialTasks.description: Update `claude_mux.go` `start` command to run an actual persistent event loop that binds the `Pool`, `Guard`, `MCPGuard`, and `Panel`. Ensure it does not exit prematurely.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement `submit` to queue tasks
sequentialTasks.description: Rewrite `submit` subcommand to execute an RPC call to the daemon's socket, passing the task description. Block on acknowledgment of receipt.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement concrete `claude` execution bindings
sequentialTasks.description: Integrate the logic into the Go binary to physically start `ollama launch claude` (or the local binary) via a PTY pipe. Connect local PTY stdout to `Parser` and local PTY stdin to orchestration signals.

sequentialTasks.task: Expose integrated TUI mode via CLI
sequentialTasks.description: Provide a CLI flag or command (`osm claude-mux tui` or natively bind to `start`) that renders the built-in JS/Go `Panel` to the local terminal, enabling direct interaction.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement `mcp parent` or configure isolated MCP endpoints
sequentialTasks.description: Implement the logic described in `T037-T038` to handle auto-configuration of `.claude.json` tool-servers mapping back to `osm mcp` for each isolated Claude Code session.

sequentialTasks.task: End-to-End Verification of CLI functionality
sequentialTasks.description: Run `osm claude-mux start` in background, `osm claude-mux submit "task"` from another terminal, and `osm claude-mux tui` from another, asserting all interact correctly. Verify via automated integration test.

sequentialTasks.task: Rule of Two Strict Review
sequentialTasks.description: Validate the entire implementation using two contiguous issue-free subagent reviews per the `strict-review-gate` protocol.

sequentialTasks.task: T112: Wire Panel TUI into claudemux run
sequentialTasks.description: DONE: Wired claudemux.Panel as multi-pane output tracker for run subcommand. Panel created in run() after pool setup, one pane per task (capped at 9 for Alt+1..9). dispatchTask routes output via AppendOutput, sets pane health running/stopped/error. OnGuardAction updates Panel health in real-time with cumulative ErrorCount. StatusBar printed in summary. Graceful degradation for >9 tasks. 5 new tests (StatusBarInOutput, PaneCountMatchesTasks, OutputRoutedToPanes, HealthErrorOnCrash, MixedHealthStates) + 2 updated. Build GREEN.

sequentialTasks.task: T113: Integration tests for claudemux run end-to-end
sequentialTasks.description: DONE: 8 integration tests + 2 new mock agent types (blockedAgent, sendErrorAgent). Tests: PoolConcurrency (max-concurrent respects pool-size), CRLFOutputHandling (Windows \r\n), SendError (broken pipe), ExceedMaxPanes (12 tasks caps at 9), ErrorOutputGuardEvent (error pattern through pipeline), LargeSequentialBatch (8 serial tasks), InstanceRegistryCreatesPerTask, AllSubsystems (capstone: verbose+crash+echo). Build GREEN.

sequentialTasks.task: T114: Auto-navigate model selection in claudemux run
sequentialTasks.description: DONE: Added tryNavigateModel to dispatchTask output loop. Sliding window buffer (menuWindowSize=20) of recent PTY lines feeds ParseModelMenu. When target model appears in parsed menu, NavigateToModel generates keystrokes sent via agent.Send(). Once-per-task modelNavigated guard prevents repeats. Key safety: requires target model in parsed menu before navigating (prevents premature single-model auto-select on partial menus). 5 tests with modelMenuAgent mock: AutoSelectsModel, NoModelFlag_SkipsDetection, ModelNotFound_SkipsNavigation, SingleModelAutoSelects, NavigateOncePerTask. Build GREEN.

sequentialTasks.task: T115: Implement osm mcp make subcommand
sequentialTasks.description: Port mcp-server-make functionality: implement 'osm mcp make' that provides MCP tools for running Make targets. Target caching, cross-platform make detection (make vs gmake), session integration. MCP tool: make_run with target and workdir params. Shell completion for make targets. Tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T116: Agent steering — session control socket (Unix/TCP)
sequentialTasks.description: Session-tied Unix socket (gRPC), Windows TCP fallback. Proto definitions: EnqueueTask, InterruptCurrent, GetStatus. Socket path: ~/.osm/sessions/<id>/control.sock. Proper cleanup on session end. Tests with in-process gRPC server. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T117: Agent steering — gRPC service implementation
sequentialTasks.description: Implement the gRPC service for EnqueueTask (adds task to session queue), InterruptCurrent (sends cancellation to active task), GetStatus (returns session state, active task, queue depth). Wire into session lifecycle. Tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T118: Implement osm mcp parent — Claude Code wrapper
sequentialTasks.description: Create 'osm mcp parent' command: wrap Claude Code child process with stdio MCP transport. Session socket messaging (T116-T117). Lifecycle management: spawn, monitor, restart on crash. MCP tools: enqueue, interrupt, status. Tests with mock Claude process. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T119: Implement osm mcp prompt — polymorphic terminal wrapper
sequentialTasks.description: Create 'osm mcp prompt': Anthropic computer use research (document findings first). Dynamic tool availability based on context. BubbleTea UI for interactive prompting. Mode detection (interactive vs pipe). go-prompt REPL integration. Adaptive MCP schema generation. Tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T120: Cross-platform checkpoint — post MCP/claudemux activation
sequentialTasks.description: Verify ALL packages pass on macOS + Linux (Docker via make-all-in-container) + Windows (host moo via make-all-run-windows). Zero failures. Fix any regressions from T101-T119. Run make on all platforms.

sequentialTasks.task: T121: CHANGELOG — MCP and claudemux activation milestone
sequentialTasks.description: Update CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] with entries for T101-T119. Keep-a-Changelog 1.1.0 format. Added/Changed/Fixed sections. Verify format. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T122: go-git v6 dependency setup
sequentialTasks.description: Add github.com/go-git/go-git/v6 to go.mod. Verify compatibility. Create internal/gitops/gogit.go with thin wrapper around go-git API for clone, add, commit, push, pull operations. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T123: Migrate sync.go to go-git for clone/add/commit/push
sequentialTasks.description: Replace exec.Command('git', ...) shell-outs in sync.go with go-git v6 calls for: clone, add, commit, push. Preserve: push commits with 'osm sync: <RFC3339>' message. Preserve sync.local-path config key. EXCEPTION: Keep shell-out for pull --rebase (go-git rebase support is limited). Tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T124: Migrate sync_startup.go to go-git for auto-pull
sequentialTasks.description: Replace exec.Command('git', 'pull', '--rebase') in sync_startup.go with go-git where feasible. Preserve: non-blocking auto-pull on startup, conflict detection from stderr. If go-git rebase is unreliable, keep as shell-out with documentation. Tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T125: go-git migration — integration tests with real repos
sequentialTasks.description: Write integration tests that clone to temp dirs, add files, commit, push to bare repos, pull with rebase. Verify conflict detection still works. Verify timestamp messages. Verify sync.local-path. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T126: Anthropic prompt library expansion — 3 new goals
sequentialTasks.description: Add 3 new built-in goals to goal_builtin.go from Anthropic prompt library NOT already implemented (existing: morale-improver, bug-buster, code-optimizer, code-explainer, meeting-notes, pii-scrubber, prose-polisher, compare-options). Each goal needs: stateVars, hotSnippets, flagDefs, promptOptions. Integration tests for each. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T127: Security audit — require() path traversal
sequentialTasks.description: Audit require() in the Goja runtime for directory traversal attacks. Verify that require('../../etc/passwd') and similar paths are blocked. Add regression tests for all known traversal patterns (../, symlinks, null bytes). Fix any vulnerabilities. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T128: Security audit — exec injection
sequentialTasks.description: Audit osm:exec and osm:pty modules for command injection. Verify that shell metacharacters in arguments are properly escaped. Test with payloads: semicolons, pipes, backticks, $(), etc. Fix any vulnerabilities. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T129: Security audit — fetch SSRF prevention
sequentialTasks.description: Audit osm:fetch for SSRF vulnerabilities. Verify that localhost, 169.254.169.254 (AWS metadata), internal IPs, file:// URLs are blocked or configurable. Add tests. Fix vulnerabilities. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T130: Security audit — environment variable secrets
sequentialTasks.description: Audit all points where environment variables are exposed to JS scripts. Verify sensitive vars (API keys, tokens, passwords) are filtered. Check MCP privilege escalation vectors. Update docs/security.md with complete threat model. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T131: Security audit — os module write restrictions
sequentialTasks.description: Audit osm:os module for unrestricted filesystem writes. Verify sandbox boundaries. Test symlink escape, /tmp races, write to sensitive paths. Fix vulnerabilities. Regression tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T132: Module resolution refinement
sequentialTasks.description: Review and refine the JS module resolution strategy (script.module-paths config key, require() in Goja). Ensure consistent behavior: relative paths resolve from script dir, absolute paths work, module-paths are searched in order. Document the resolution algorithm in docs/scripting.md. Add edge case tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.description: Audit all config keys in internal/config/schema.go. Verify each key is: (1) documented in docs/reference/config.md, (2) actually read and used in the codebase, (3) has correct default values, (4) has validation. Remove any dead config keys. Add missing documentation. Run make.

sequentialTasks.description: Review and refine the code review splitter in internal/command/diff_splitter.go. Ensure it handles all git diff formats correctly. Test with large diffs, binary files, renames, mode changes. Improve splitting heuristics. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T174: Final Rule of Two commit gate
sequentialTasks.description: Execute Rule of Two: subagent #1 reviews full diff vs main. If PASS: freeze scope, subagent #2 reviews same diff. Both PASS: commit with Kubernetes-style message. Either FAIL: fix, reset counter to 0, restart gate.

Areas to consider: performance optimization, new JS modules, additional goals, UI/UX refinements, new MCP tools, telemetry, plugin system. The blueprint must NEVER be empty.

continuousVerification.task: Run 'make make-all-with-log' after EVERY significant change
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY test fails, STOP and fix. Zero tolerance.
continuousVerification.testCoverage: Target 100%. Verify with go tool cover.
continuousVerification.crossPlatform: Checkpoints at T120, T136, T160, T170-T172.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: Use config.mk custom targets. Limit output. Tee to build.log.
No timing-dependent tests. No long-running tests.
Continually refine. After every task fix stale descriptions.
Two contiguous reviews. Same diff. Reset on any code change.
NEVER add .deadcodeignore entries. Wire up or delete.
Run make after EVERY change. Zero failures before proceeding.
